prose: Ferrocene Fukui reactivity indices were computed using GFN2-xTB in the gas phase at charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral singlet ground state). The N+1 cation and N−1 anion were computed at multiplicity 2 per the Fukui formalism. The geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz file (21 atoms: Fe, two C₅H₅ rings). All three charge states converged successfully; charge conservation checks (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000) passed. The iron center is the most electrophilic (f⁺ = 0.22885456) and most nucleophilic (f⁻ = 0.25938191) site, indicating it is the primary reactive center. The dual descriptor at Fe is negative (−0.03053), predicting preference for electron removal, consistent with ferrocene's redox activity. Carbon atoms show low Fukui indices (~0.013), and hydrogen atoms show intermediate values (~0.063). A Fukui reactivity visualization plot was generated and saved. Calculation integrity status: ok, trustworthy.
